author: śrīla premānanda⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
ore mana! ki bhaya śamane kari āra
jadi kṛṣṇa-pade rati, ki karibe pitṛ⋅pati,
ihā kene nā kara vicāra

je pada bharasā kari’, brahmā sṛṣṭi⋅adhikārī,
je⋅pada vāñchaye pañcānana
je pade gaṅgāra janma, lakṣmī jāne jā’ra marma,
aharniśi smare anukṣaṇa

dhruva⋅ādi je prasāde, jogīndra dharaye hṛde,
munigaṇa je⋅pada dheyāya
draupadī prahlāda kari, je⋅pada hṛdaye smari,
dekha kata saṅkaṭa eḍāya

jadi kara nija kāja, mitra habe dharmarāja,
vṛthā cinta asāra⋅saṁsāra
kahe dīna premānanda, cinta kṛṣṇa⋅padadvandva,
tribhuvane śatru nahe āra
en_translation:
O my mind! What further fear do you have of the God of Death? If you have deep attachment for the lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, what can the Lord of the Dead do to you? Why do you not consider this?

Relying on those lotus feet, Lord Brahmā became the master of creation. Those same feet are desired by the five-faced Lord Śiva. From those feet the River Ganges was born, and the Goddess of Fortune knows their true essence and remembers them day and night.

By the grace of those feet, Dhruva Mahārāja and the great kings among the yogīs hold them in their hearts, and all the sages meditate upon them. Consider how many dangers were averted by Draupadī and Prahlāda Mahārāja just by remembering those lotus feet.

If you perform your true duty of devotion, the King of Death will become your friend. You are worrying uselessly about this worthless material world. The humble Premānanda says: "Meditate upon the twin lotus feet of Lord Kṛṣṇa, and you will have no more enemies in the three worlds."
